previous_headings: 1. Package Purpose and Dual Role
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: package serves two distinct roles simultaneously: working R package demonstrates runtime SIMD dispatch (exporting count_nonzero, simd_backend, etc.). scaffolding/template tool downstream packages copy via use_simd_dispatch(). exported R functions simd_dispatch_template_path() use_simd_dispatch() developer-tooling functions belong normal user-facing package API. clutter namespace confusing end users install package dispatch demonstration rather scaffolding dependency.

previous_headings: 1. Package Purpose and Dual Role
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Keep simd_dispatch_template_path() use_simd_dispatch() dedicated template helper file mark clearly developer utilities. depend usethis runtime; helper update package files directly document functions package authors, end users.

previous_headings: 2. Template Strategy > 2.1 Template is a static copy with no placeholder substitution
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: inst/templates/dispatch-c/ near-verbatim duplicate src/ R/. template files contain placeholders. README says “rename rsd_ RC_ symbols”, entirely manual. Concrete strings downstream adopter must find-replace hand: rename parameter, sed substitution, checklist. Adopters routinely miss R_init_RsimdDispatch registration.c, producing silent broken DLL.

previous_headings: 2. Template Strategy > 2.1 Template is a static copy with no placeholder substitution
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Add pkg argument use_simd_dispatch() perform token substitution copy: Also ship inst/templates/dispatch-c/CHECKLIST.md enumerating every substitution point users copy manually.
code: use_simd_dispatch <- function(path = ".", pkg = basename(normalizePath(path)),                                prefix = tolower(pkg), overwrite = FALSE) {   template <- simd_dispatch_template_path()   path <- normalizePath(path, mustWork = TRUE)   files <- list.files(template, recursive = TRUE, all.files = TRUE,                       no.. = TRUE, full.names = FALSE)   # Only file entries are returned by list.files; remove dead dir.exists branch   copied <- character()   for (file in files) {     from <- file.path(template, file)     to   <- file.path(path, file)     dir.create(dirname(to), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)     if (file.exists(to) && !isTRUE(overwrite))       stop("refusing to overwrite existing file: ", to, call. = FALSE)     txt <- readLines(from, warn = FALSE)     txt <- gsub("RsimdDispatch", pkg,    txt, fixed = TRUE)     txt <- gsub("rsd_",          paste0(prefix, "_"), txt, fixed = TRUE)     txt <- gsub("RC_",           paste0(toupper(prefix), "_C_"), txt, fixed = TRUE)     writeLines(txt, to)     copied <- c(copied, to)   }   invisible(copied) }

previous_headings: 2. Template Strategy > 2.2 Template duplicates live source — no sync mechanism
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: inst/templates/dispatch-c/src/simd_dispatch.c byte--byte identical src/simd_dispatch.c. holds cpu_features.c/h, kernel_*.c, r_api.c, registration.c, Makevars., configure, configure.win, configure-simd-dispatch.sh. bug fixed live src/ must manually mirrored template. CI step detects drift.

previous_headings: 2. Template Strategy > 2.2 Template duplicates live source — no sync mechanism
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Add Makefile target CI check diffs two trees: Add CI R-CMD-check.yaml pre-check step: Longer term, generate template src/ build time (applying rsd_ → @PREFIX@ substitution), one source truth.
code: check-template-sync:     @diff -rq --exclude="*.o" --exclude="*.so" --exclude="Makevars" \         src/ inst/templates/dispatch-c/src/ \         || (echo "ERROR: template/src drift detected" && exit 1) - name: Check template sync   run: make check-template-sync

previous_headings: 2. Template Strategy > 2.4 use_simd_dispatch() dead code — dir.exists(from) branch unreachable
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: R/dispatch.R:96: list.files(recursive = TRUE) returns file paths, never directory entries. branch never entered.
code: if (dir.exists(from)) {   dir.create(to, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   next }

previous_headings: 2. Template Strategy > 2.4 use_simd_dispatch() dead code — dir.exists(from) branch unreachable
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Remove dead branch. dir.create(dirname(), ...) call line already handles creating needed intermediate directories.
code: # Before (dead branch present): if (dir.exists(from)) {   dir.create(to, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)   next } dir.create(dirname(to), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  # After: dir.create(dirname(to), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.1 SIMDE_INCLUDE hardcoded to inst/include
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh:37: correct RsimdDispatch , template ships identical line. downstream package relying RsimdDispatch’s vendored SIMDe via LinkingTo SIMDe headers R library tree, inst/include. check_cflag probes empty main (see §3.2), flag detection silently succeeds regardless, downstream package fails compile include path absent.
code: SIMDE_INCLUDE="-I$ROOT/inst/include"

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.1 SIMDE_INCLUDE hardcoded to inst/include
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Make SIMDe include path configurable sensible auto-detect fallback: Document RSD_SIMDE_INCLUDE overrideable environment variable template README.
code: # In configure-simd-dispatch.sh, replace the hardcoded line: SIMDE_INCLUDE="-I$ROOT/inst/include"  # With: if [ -z "${RSD_SIMDE_INCLUDE:-}" ]; then     if [ -d "$ROOT/inst/include/simde" ]; then         RSD_SIMDE_INCLUDE="-I$ROOT/inst/include"     elif [ -n "${R_HOME:-}" ]; then         # Try LinkingTo path from an installed RsimdDispatch         _rsd_inc=$("${R_HOME}/bin/Rscript" -e \             'cat(system.file("include", package="RsimdDispatch"))' 2>/dev/null || true)         RSD_SIMDE_INCLUDE=${_rsd_inc:+"-I${_rsd_inc}"}     fi fi SIMDE_INCLUDE=${RSD_SIMDE_INCLUDE:-""}

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.2 check_cflag does not include SIMDe headers in the probe
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: probe file empty #include. tests compiler accepts -m flag, SIMDe compiles . SIMDe headers trigger errors specific flag combinations strict warning flags.
code: cat > "$CONFDIR/conftest.c" <<'EOF' int main(void) { return 0; } EOF

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.2 check_cflag does not include SIMDe headers in the probe
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Include relevant SIMDe header probe:
code: check_cflag() {     flags=$1     header=${2:-}     {         if [ -n "$header" ]; then             printf '#include <%s>\n' "$header"         fi         printf 'int main(void) { return 0; }\n'     } > "$CONFDIR/conftest.c"     # shellcheck disable=SC2086     ${CC} ${CPPFLAGS:-} ${CFLAGS:-} ${SIMDE_INCLUDE} ${flags} \         -c "$CONFDIR/conftest.c" -o "$CONFDIR/conftest.o" >/dev/null 2>&1 }  # Usage: if check_cflag "-mavx2" "simde/x86/avx2.h"; then ...

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.3 uname -m detects host arch, not compiler target
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: cross-compilation host (e.g. building arm64 macOS x86_64 Linux), uname -m returns host arch enables wrong backend set. CRAN’s arm64 macOS builders can exercise path.
code: arch=$(uname -m 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.3 uname -m detects host arch, not compiler target
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Query compiler target triple first, fall back uname -m: -dumpmachine supported GCC Clang returns target triple regardless host. Strip everything first - get just CPU name.
code: arch=$( ${CC} -dumpmachine 2>/dev/null | sed 's/-.*//' || uname -m 2>/dev/null || echo unknown )

title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: configure-simd-dispatch.sh:139: template copied MyPkg, configure output confusingly says "RsimdDispatch configure:" installation.
code: echo "RsimdDispatch configure: objects='kernel_scalar.o ${RSD_OBJECTS}'" echo "RsimdDispatch configure: wrote ${CONFIG_OUT} and ${MAKEVARS_OUT}"

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.4 Log message hardcodes package name
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Make log prefix configurable via environment variable default: template’s configure script, set:
code: RSD_LOG_PREFIX=${RSD_LOG_PREFIX:-RsimdDispatch} # ... echo "${RSD_LOG_PREFIX} configure: objects='kernel_scalar.o ${RSD_OBJECTS}'" echo "${RSD_LOG_PREFIX} configure: wrote ${CONFIG_OUT} and ${MAKEVARS_OUT}" RSD_LOG_PREFIX="$(basename "$ROOT")" \ RSD_PACKAGE_ROOT="$ROOT" \ ... sh "$ROOT/tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh"

text: configure.win passes RSD_MAKEVARS_OUT=src/Makevars.win still reads src/Makevars.. works today content platform-identical. Windows-specific compiler flags ever needed (MSVC /arch:AVX2 vs GCC -mavx2, PE export attributes, etc.), clean path diverge.

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.5 No Makevars.win.in — asymmetric configure pair
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Add src/Makevars.win.initially identical src/Makevars., configure.win explicitly request : Update cleanup Makefile clean also remove src/Makevars.win.
code: # configure.win RSD_PACKAGE_ROOT="$ROOT" \ RSD_MAKEVARS_IN=src/Makevars.win.in \ RSD_MAKEVARS_OUT=src/Makevars.win \ sh "$ROOT/tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh"

title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Object list built string concatenation: ROOT TMPDIR contains spaces, check_cflag invocation silently misinterprets word-split arguments, causing flag checks return false (everything falls back scalar), warning emitted.
code: RSD_OBJECTS="$RSD_OBJECTS $1"

previous_headings: 3. Configure Script Issues > 3.6 append_object breaks when paths contain spaces
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Quote conftest paths consistently validate ROOT script entry: conftest , ensure paths quoted — existing "$CONFDIR/conftest.c" correct ${SIMDE_INCLUDE} flag variables unquoted compiler invocation. Since intentionally word-split (multiple flags), acceptable, $ROOT SIMDE_INCLUDE must contain spaces.
code: # Validate early case "$ROOT" in     *\ *) echo "WARNING: package root contains spaces; SIMD detection may be unreliable" >&2 ;; esac

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.1 Single kernel hardcoded throughout
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: simd_dispatch.h exposes exactly one dispatchable function: Every backend file, dispatch table, R API coupled one signature. package wanting dispatch multiple kernels different signatures must duplicate entire infrastructure.
code: typedef size_t (*rsd_count_nonzero_fn)(const uint8_t *x, size_t n); size_t rsd_count_nonzero(const uint8_t *x, size_t n);

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.1 Single kernel hardcoded throughout
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Document explicitly template README distinct kernel signature requires simd_dispatch_<name>.c / simd_dispatch_<name>.h pair, provide skeleton second kernel. stronger solution, introduce X-macro table lists backends generates switch logic: rsd_backend_known, rsd_backend_compiled, rsd_backend_cpu_supported become: reduces 10-location update problem single backends.def edit.
code: /* backends.def — included by simd_dispatch.c, cpu_features.c, etc. */ /* X(name, have_macro, cpu_check_fn) */ X(scalar,  1,              1) X(sse2,    RSD_HAVE_SSE2,  rsd_cpu_has_sse2()) X(sse41,   RSD_HAVE_SSE41, rsd_cpu_has_sse41()) X(avx2,    RSD_HAVE_AVX2,  rsd_cpu_has_avx2()) X(avx512,  RSD_HAVE_AVX512,rsd_cpu_has_avx512()) X(neon,    RSD_HAVE_NEON,  rsd_cpu_has_neon()) int rsd_backend_known(const char *b) { #define X(name, have, cpu) if (strcmp(b, #name) == 0) return 1; #include "backends.def" #undef X     return 0; }

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.2 rsd_backends_csv rotating static buffers are not thread-safe
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Concurrent callers race next. R’s single-threaded model usually harmless, functions also exported template downstream use threading guarantees differ.
code: static char bufs[3][128]; static int next = 0;

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.2 rsd_backends_csv rotating static buffers are not thread-safe
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Since RC_simd_info (public caller) already builds proper STRSXP character vectors directly, CSV functions redundant public API. Mark static (internal linkage) remove . must remain diagnostics/debugging, replace caller-supplied buffers:
code: /* Caller owns the buffer; no static state. */ void rsd_compiled_backends_csv(char *out, size_t cap);

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.3 Dispatch is not thread-safe
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: memory barrier atomic around reads writes. fork()ed worker (parallel::mclapply) inherits initialized pointer safe, worker races simd_set_backend() rsd_count_nonzero() may read torn pointer.
code: static rsd_count_nonzero_fn rsd_count_nonzero_impl = rsd_count_nonzero_scalar;

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.3 Dispatch is not thread-safe
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: C11 targets, use _Atomic: pre-C11 portability, add volatile annotation document limitation. template, note simd_set_backend() safe call concurrently kernel.
code: #include <stdatomic.h> static _Atomic rsd_count_nonzero_fn rsd_count_nonzero_impl; /* write: */ atomic_store(&rsd_count_nonzero_impl, fn); /* read:  */ return atomic_load(&rsd_count_nonzero_impl)(x, n);

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.4 Backend name buffer size is a magic number
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: size 32 appears three times across file named constant. backend name longer 31 bytes silently truncated.
code: static char rsd_requested_backend_buf[32] = "auto"; static char rsd_selected_backend_buf[32] = "uninitialized";

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.4 Backend name buffer size is a magic number
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Also add static_assert (C11) compile-time sizeof check verify longest known backend name fits:
code: #define RSD_BACKEND_NAME_MAX 32 static char rsd_requested_backend_buf[RSD_BACKEND_NAME_MAX] = "auto"; static char rsd_selected_backend_buf[RSD_BACKEND_NAME_MAX] = "uninitialized"; /* snprintf calls use sizeof(rsd_requested_backend_buf) already — keep as-is */ /* longest current name: "uninitialized" = 13 chars */ typedef char rsd_backend_name_size_check[     (RSD_BACKEND_NAME_MAX > 13) ? 1 : -1];

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.5 simd_dispatch.c includes <R.h> only for Rf_error
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: dispatch layer depends R’s error mechanism, making non-portable non-R C callers (unit test harnesses, embedding, etc.).

previous_headings: 4. C Dispatch Layer Generality > 4.5 simd_dispatch.c includes <R.h> only for Rf_error
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Add pluggable error callback: r_api.c (R-specific layer), register Rf_error handler R_init_<PKGNAME>. keeps dispatch core free R headers.
code: /* In simd_dispatch.h */ typedef void (*rsd_error_fn)(const char *msg); void rsd_set_error_handler(rsd_error_fn fn);  /* NULL = use default (abort) */

previous_headings: 5. R Layer Issues > 5.1 simd_set_backend() validates in R and again in C redundantly
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: dispatch.R:39 uses match.arg() rejects unknown strings. C function independently validates rsd_backend_known, rsd_backend_compiled, rsd_backend_cpu_supported. R-side list must kept sync C-side list hand.

previous_headings: 5. R Layer Issues > 5.1 simd_set_backend() validates in R and again in C redundantly
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Either remove R-side match.arg rely C error (already produces clear message), generate R-side vector simd_info()$compiled_backends package load time: way valid set always sync actually compiled.
code: simd_set_backend <- function(backend = "auto") {   backend <- match.arg(backend, c("auto", simd_info()$compiled_backends))   invisible(.Call(RC_simd_set_backend, backend)) }

previous_headings: 5. R Layer Issues > 5.2 count_nonzero returns double instead of integer
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: r_api.c:31: return Rf_ScalarReal((double)count). typical vectors count fits 32-bit integer. Returning double forces downstream comparisons like count_nonzero(x) == length(x) use floating-point equality.

previous_headings: 5. R Layer Issues > 5.2 count_nonzero returns double instead of integer
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Return integer count fits, double otherwise: consistently return double document choice explicitly. current behaviour minimum documented roxygen @return tag.
code: if (count <= (size_t)INT_MAX) {     return Rf_ScalarInteger((int)count); } return Rf_ScalarReal((double)count);

previous_headings: 6. CI and Build System > 6.3 No CI installation test for the template
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Add dedicated workflow (step existing check) :
code: - name: Install copied template package   run: |     Rscript -e '       tmp <- file.path(tempdir(), "TestSIMD")       dir.create(tmp)       writeLines(c(         "Package: TestSIMD", "Version: 0.0.1", "License: GPL-2"       ), file.path(tmp, "DESCRIPTION"))       RsimdDispatch::use_simd_dispatch(tmp, pkg = "TestSIMD")     '     R CMD INSTALL "$TMPDIR/TestSIMD"

previous_headings: 6. CI and Build System > 6.4 make build always regenerates inst/AUTHORS
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: update-authors runs network-touching tools/update-authors.R every make build, modifying inst/AUTHORS. makes builds non-reproducible upstream author list changes runs.
code: build: update-authors

previous_headings: 6. CI and Build System > 6.4 make build always regenerates inst/AUTHORS
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Decouple author regeneration build: Run make authors explicitly CRAN submission. Commit resulting inst/AUTHORS file version control build reproducible without network call.
code: build:     R CMD build .  authors: update-authors  update-authors:     Rscript tools/update-authors.R

previous_headings: 7. Minor / Style
what: 7.1 NEON kernel uses lane-store loop instead of horizontal add
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: kernel_neon.c use rsd_popcount horizontal NEON add: loop correct slower vaddvq_u8 (available AArch64) pair vpaddlq_u8 / vaddlvq_u16. intentional 32-bit ARM compatibility worth documenting. Fix: Add comment noting lane-store loop used instead vaddvq_u8, add #ifdef __aarch64__ fast path AArch64.
code: simde_vst1q_u8(lanes, eq); for (int j = 0; j < 16; ++j) {     acc += lanes[j] == 0;  /* note: eq byte is 0xFF when equal, 0x00 when not */ }

previous_headings: 7. Minor / Style
what: 7.2 kernel_neon.c missing kernel_common.h dependency in Makevars.in
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: Makevars.lists kernel_common.h explicit dependency x86 kernels kernel_neon.c kernel_scalar.c. technically correct today (neither file includes kernel_common.h), either file gains kernel_common.h include future, make rebuild . Consistent listing aids correctness. Fix: Either add kernel_common.h kernel_neon.o kernel_scalar.o rules, add comment explaining omission intentional.

previous_headings: 7. Minor / Style
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: simd_dispatch.c:224–230: want_available branch checked first even though rsd_compiled_backends_csv passes (1,0,0) rsd_available_backends_csv passes (0,0,1). logic produces correct results, flag priority order (available > compiled > supported) inverse callers suggest, three flags mutually exclusive, latent bug caller passes (1,0,1). Fix: Replace three booleans single enum replace function three distinct static helpers, one per query type.
code: if (want_available) {     include = rsd_backend_available(name); } else if (want_compiled) {     include = rsd_backend_compiled(name); } else if (want_supported) {     include = rsd_backend_cpu_supported(name); }

previous_headings: 7. Minor / Style
what: 7.4 Template configure-simd-dispatch.sh in tools/ but accessed as $ROOT/tools/...
title: RsimdDispatch — Design Review and Proposed Fixes
text: template’s configure script calls sh "$ROOT/tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh". template copied downstream package already tools/ directory, script path correct. However, downstream package tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh (previous copy), diverged version may executed silently. path documented explicitly.

title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: SIMD source files use SIMDe types functions, backend names refer native target-specific translation units. configuration, optional backend accepted compiler flag SIMDe header together define expected native SIMDe macro. example, AVX2 probe compiles -mavx2, includes <simde/x86/avx2.h>, requires SIMDE_X86_AVX2_NATIVE. AVX-512 similarly requires native F, BW, VL macros. flags make compiler define target macros __AVX2__ __AVX512BW__. SIMDe maps SIMDE_ARCH_* SIMDE_*_NATIVE; SIMDe function bodies use native intrinsics macros fall back native macro absent. dispatcher, CPU feature detection, R API still compiled without ISA flags. installed diagnostics report backends passed SIMDe-native compile probe:
code: simd_info()[c("compiled_backends", "simde_native_backends", "simde_version", "simde_commit")] #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #>  #> $simde_native_backends #> [1] "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #>  #> $simde_version #> [1] "0.8.4" #>  #> $simde_commit #> [1] "f3e8262173b7089db9a9d57a9ecef8dd07ad9c97"

title: Runtime Dispatch Semantics
text: following benchmark evaluated article built. uses small input package checks remain fast, still exercises -process backend switching public API. "auto" selects best backend compiled supported intersection. current ranking : Architecture guards mean x86 systems normally consider x86 backends ARM systems normally consider NEON. Scalar always available.
code: if (requireNamespace("bench", quietly = TRUE)) {   bench_x <- rep(as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 3, 0, 255, 7, 0)), length.out = 2^20)    bench <- bench::mark(     scalar = {       simd_set_backend("scalar")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     auto = {       simd_set_backend("auto")       count_nonzero(bench_x)     },     iterations = 5,     check = TRUE   )    simd_set_backend("auto")   bench[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "n_itr")] } #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   expression   median `itr/sec` #>   <bch:expr> <bch:tm>     <dbl> #> 1 scalar      471.9µs     2107. #> 2 auto         55.6µs    16866. avx512 > avx2 > sse41 > sse2 > neon > scalar

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: downstream package root: pkg omitted, package name read DESCRIPTION. prefix omitted, lowercase C identifier prefix derived package name. helper performs important substitutions, including @useDynLib, R_init_<Package>(), rsd_, RSD_, RC_ symbols. copied scaffold includes:
code: RsimdDispatch::use_simd_dispatch(pkg = "MyPackage", prefix = "mypkg") configure configure.win cleanup tools/configure-simd-dispatch.sh src/Makevars.in src/Makevars.win.in src/cpu_features.c src/simd_dispatch.c src/kernel_scalar.c src/kernel_avx2.c src/kernel_avx512.c src/kernel_neon.c

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: demo kernel shape: real package, replace demo kernel signature keep structure: put -mavx2, -mavx512*, -march=native global package flags. dispatcher must remain safe baseline CPUs.
code: typedef size_t (*rsd_count_nonzero_fn)(const uint8_t *x, size_t n); R API wrapper        baseline flags CPU feature checks   baseline flags dispatch table       baseline flags scalar kernel        baseline flags SSE/AVX/NEON files   per-file ISA flags

title: Using RsimdDispatch in Other Packages
text: generated configure checks compiler support writes src/config.h plus src/Makevars. Runtime CPU support checked later installed package:
code: R CMD INSTALL .

title: Authors and Citation

what: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Pure-C runtime SIMD dispatch templates R packages. Compile scalar, SSE, AVX, AVX-512, NEON C translation units separately; switch among compiled CPU-supported implementations runtime without unsafe overrides.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: GitHub:
code: install.packages(   "RsimdDispatch",   repos = c("https://sounkou-bioinfo.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("sounkou-bioinfo/RsimdDispatch")

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Copy C dispatch scaffold package root: helper updates DESCRIPTION LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch, writes scaffold files, substitutes package-specific registration C symbol prefixes. add runtime dependency RsimdDispatch. Replace demo count_nonzero() kernels scalar/SSE/AVX/NEON kernels. Keep R API, CPU detection, dispatcher baseline compiler flags; ISA-specific translation units get flags -mavx2 -mavx512f -mavx512bw -mavx512vl. See package vignettes downstream-package workflow dispatch semantics.
code: RsimdDispatch::use_simd_dispatch(pkg = "MyPackage", prefix = "mypkg")

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: simd_set_backend() rejects uncompiled CPU-unsupported backends. unsafe override.
code: library(RsimdDispatch)  x <- as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 0, 255))  count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_backend() #> [1] "scalar" simd_set_backend("avx2") simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("auto") simd_info()[c("compiled_backends", "cpu_supported_backends", "available_backends", "simde_native_backends")] #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #>  #> $cpu_supported_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   #>  #> $simde_native_backends #> [1] "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" simde_info()[c("version", "commit")] #> $version #> [1] "0.8.4" #>  #> $commit #> [1] "f3e8262173b7089db9a9d57a9ecef8dd07ad9c97" simd_dispatch_template_path() #> [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/site-library/RsimdDispatch/templates/dispatch-c"

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Switching backends -process variants linked one R shared library selected guarded function pointers.
code: count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 simd_info()[c("selected_backend", "available_backends")] #> $selected_backend #> [1] "avx2" #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") scalar_count <- count_nonzero(x)  simd_set_backend("avx2") avx2_count <- count_nonzero(x)  simd_set_backend("auto") data.frame(   scalar = scalar_count,   avx2 = avx2_count,   selected_after_auto = simd_backend() ) #>   scalar avx2 selected_after_auto #> 1      3    3                avx2

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
code: set.seed(1) x <- as.raw(sample.int(256L, 50 * 1024 * 1024, replace = TRUE) - 1L)  mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     count_nonzero(x)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     count_nonzero(x)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(mark$median) * 1000,   mb_per_second = length(x) * as.numeric(mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- bench$mb_per_second / bench$mb_per_second[bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(bench, digits = 3)

what: Count non-zero bytes with the selected SIMD backend — count_nonzero
title: Count non-zero bytes with the selected SIMD backend — count_nonzero
text: Demonstration kernel runtime dispatch template. count_nonzero() counts bytes 00 raw vector using currently selected backend. default backend "auto", selects best compiled backend supported current CPU/runtime.

what: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
title: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
text: use_simd_dispatch() copies dispatch scaffold R package performs package-name C-prefix substitutions needed working package. writes package files, updates DESCRIPTION, .Rbuildignore, .gitignore, returns copied paths invisibly.

title: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
code: simd_dispatch_template_path()  use_simd_dispatch(   path = ".",   pkg = NULL,   prefix = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
text: path Package root template copied. pkg R package name. NULL, name read DESCRIPTION. prefix C symbol prefix used replace rsd_ copied sources. default sanitized lowercase package name. overwrite Whether overwrite existing files. quiet Whether suppress progress messages.

title: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
text: Invisibly returns copied file paths.

title: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
text: function intended package authors. needed runtime users packages already include generated dispatch code.

title: Configure an R package for C runtime SIMD dispatch — simd_dispatch_template_path
code: simd_dispatch_template_path() #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/RsimdDispatch/templates/dispatch-c"

what: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: Returns requested backend, selected backend, compiled backends, CPU-supported backends, SIMDe-native backends, target information, SIMDe provenance compiled shared library. Calling initializes lazy auto-dispatch selection already initialized.

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
code: simd_info()

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: named list dispatch CPU feature diagnostics. Backend-set entries character vectors, comma-separated strings.

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
code: names(simd_info()) #>  [1] "dispatch_mode"          "requested_backend"      "selected_backend"       #>  [4] "compiled_backends"      "cpu_supported_backends" "available_backends"     #>  [7] "simde_native_backends"  "cpu_sse2"               "cpu_sse41"              #> [10] "cpu_avx2"               "cpu_avx512"             "cpu_neon"               #> [13] "target_arch"            "target_os"              "simde_version"          #> [16] "simde_commit"

what: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: Select backend used subsequent calls count_nonzero(). RsimdDispatch keeps compiled variants one shared object switches guarded function pointers. makes -process benchmarking possible.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
code: simd_set_backend(   backend = c("auto", "scalar", "sse2", "sse41", "avx2", "avx512", "neon") )

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: backend One "auto", "scalar", "sse2", "sse41", "avx2", "avx512", "neon".

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: selected backend, invisibly. "auto", backend chosen compiled CPU-supported set.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
code: old <- simd_backend() simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_set_backend("auto")

code: simde_info()[c("version", "commit")] #> $version #> [1] "0.8.4" #>  #> $commit #> [1] "f3e8262173b7089db9a9d57a9ecef8dd07ad9c97" #>

title: RsimdDispatch 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Provides working runtime SIMD dispatch example R packages, using one shared library scalar, SSE2, SSE4.1, AVX2, AVX-512, NEON translation units supported compiler CPU. Exports count_nonzero(), simd_set_backend(), simd_backend(), simd_info() demonstrate safe -process backend selection diagnostics. Adds use_simd_dispatch() simd_dispatch_template_path() package authors want copy adapt C dispatch scaffold. Vendors header-SIMDe library downstream packages LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch. Adds simde_info() SIMDe fields simd_info() report vendored SIMDe version pinned upstream commit.
